name: T-Rex
description: Carnivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: T. rex was first discovered in 1902 by Barnum Brown in the Hell Creek Formation of Montana.
palaeobiology_info: T. rex had massive jaw strength and was built for hunting large herbivorous dinosaurs. Its keen senses made it a formidable hunter.

name: Spinosaurus
description: Pescivore that lived in Northern Africa.
discovery_info: Spinosaurus fossils were first described in 1915 by Ernst Stromer.
palaeobiology_info: The only known semi-aquatic dinosaur. Its large dorsal sail may have been used for display or thermoregulation.

name: Stegosaurus
description: Herbivore that lived in the United States and Portugal.
discovery_info: Stegosaurus was first discovered in 1877 in the Morrison Formation of Colorado.
palaeobiology_info: Stegosaurus’s plates were likely for display or thermoregulation, while the thagomizer was for defense.

name: Triceratops
description: Herbivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Triceratops was first discovered in 1887 by Othniel Charles Marsh in Denver, Colorado.
palaeobiology_info: A large bony frill protecting its neck and three large horns used in defense and social displays.

name: Ankylosaurus
description: Herbivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Ankylosaurus was first discovered in 1906 by Peter Kaisen during an expedition from the American Museum of Natural History.
palaeobiology_info: One of the most heavily armored dinosaurs with extensive bony plating covering its body and a club-like tail.

name: Carnotaurus
description: Carnivore that lived in South America.
discovery_info: Carnotaurus was first described in 1985 after being discovered in Argentina.
palaeobiology_info: Distinctive horns above its eyes. Its robust build and strong legs made it an effective hunter.

name: Brachiosaurus
description: Herbivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Brachiosaurus was first discovered in 1900 in the Morrison Formation by Elmer S. Riggs.
palaeobiology_info: One of the largest land animals ever, with a long neck reaching high into trees for vegetation.

name: Mosasaurus
description: Carnivore that lived in the Atlantic Ocean.
discovery_info: Mosasaurus fossils have been discovered across marine deposits worldwide.
palaeobiology_info: Mosasaurus was an apex marine predator with powerful jaws, sharp teeth, and a muscular tail.

name: Woolly Mammoth
description: Herbivore that lived in North America, Asia, and Europe.
discovery_info: Woolly mammoths were described in in 1799 by Johann Friedrich Blumenbach.
palaeobiology_info: Woolly mammoths were adapted to cold climates with thick fur, small ears, and a layer of fat for insulation.

name: Pteranodon
description: Pescavore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Pteronadon was described in 1876 by Othniel Charles Marsh.
palaeobiology_info: It had a distinctive head crest and hunted fish from the air, using its long pointed beak to snatch prey from the sea.

name: Compsognathus
description: Small carnivore that lived in Europe.
discovery_info: Compsognathus was first described in 1859.
palaeobiology_info: One of the smallest known dinosaurs, fast and agile.

name: Dilophosaurus
description: Carnivore with crests that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Dilophosaurus was discovered in 1942.
palaeobiology_info: Known for its distinctive double crests on its head.

name: Dimetrodon
description: Carnivore that lived in North America.
discovery_info: Dimetrodon fossils were first found in the early 20th century.
palaeobiology_info: Not a dinosaur but a prehistoric reptile with a sail on its back.

name: Dimorphodon
description: Flying Reptile that lived in Europe.
discovery_info: Dimorphodon was described in 1828.
palaeobiology_info: A flying reptile with a wingspan of about 1.5 meters.

name: Dunkleosteus
description: Armored fish that lived in ancient seas.
discovery_info: Dunkleosteus fossils were discovered in the 19th century.
palaeobiology_info: One of the largest placoderm fishes with powerful jaws.

name: Gallimimus
description: Fast herbivore that lived in Mongolia.
discovery_info: Gallimimus was discovered in 1972.
palaeobiology_info: Known for its speed and bird-like features.

name: Ichthyosaurus
description: Marine reptile that lived in ancient seas.
discovery_info: Ichthyosaurus fossils were first described in the 19th century.
palaeobiology_info: Dolphin-like marine reptile adapted for fast swimming.

name: Megladon
description: Giant prehistoric shark.
discovery_info: Megalodon teeth fossils are common worldwide.
palaeobiology_info: One of the largest and most powerful sharks ever.

name: Pachycephalosaurus
description: Herbivore with a thick skull dome.
discovery_info: Pachycephalosaurus was discovered in 1931.
palaeobiology_info: Skull dome likely used for head-butting behavior.

name: Parasaurolophus
description: Herbivore with a long crest.
discovery_info: Parasaurolophus was first described in 1922.
palaeobiology_info: Crest may have been used for sound production or display.

name: Quetzalcoatlus
description: Largest flying reptile.
discovery_info: Quetzalcoatlus was described in 1971.
palaeobiology_info: Had a wingspan up to 10 meters, one of the largest flying animals ever.

name: Sinoceratops
description: Herbivore with a unique frill.
discovery_info: Sinoceratops was discovered in 2010.
palaeobiology_info: Distinctive frill with unique ornamentation.

name: Therizinosaurus
description: Herbivore with large claws.
discovery_info: Therizinosaurus was described in 1954.
palaeobiology_info: Known for its large, scythe-like claws.

name: Velociraptor
description: Small carnivore that lived in Mongolia.
discovery_info: Velociraptor was discovered in 1924.
palaeobiology_info: Famous for its speed and hunting in packs.

name: Archelon
description: Giant sea turtle that lived in ancient seas.
discovery_info: Archelon was first described in 1895.
palaeobiology_info: One of the largest turtles ever, with a broad shell and flippers for swimming.

name: Glyptodon
description: Large armored mammal that lived in South America.
discovery_info: Glyptodon fossils were discovered in the 19th century.
palaeobiology_info: Covered in a large, bony shell for protection.

name: Megatherium
description: Giant ground sloth that lived in South America.
discovery_info: Megatherium was described in the 18th century.
palaeobiology_info: One of the largest land mammals, slow-moving but powerful.

name: Smilodon
description: Large saber-toothed cat that lived in North and South America.
discovery_info: Smilodon fossils were first described in the 19th century.
palaeobiology_info: Known for its long, curved saber-like canine teeth.
